title: Caza Ranches LLC and Department of Homeland Security Land Exchange Act of 2025
short_title: Caza Ranches LLC and Department of Homeland Security Land Exchange Act of 2025
plain_summary: This law lets the Department of Homeland Security trade land with a private company called Caza Ranches LLC. Both pieces of land are about 160 acres and are located in Artesia, New Mexico. The government will give away some federal land and get private land in return. The private land that the government gets will be used to build training facilities for law enforcement officers.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Caza Ranches LLC and Department of Homeland Security Land Exchange Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Land Exchange
sections.text:
This section explains how the government will trade land with a private company. First, some important terms are defined. Centers means the Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers and related buildings that are located in Artesia, New Mexico. Federal land means about one hundred sixty acres of land in Artesia, New Mexico that the government currently owns. This land is identified as parcel number four dash one four nine dash zero nine six dash two six six dash four six zero. The legal description is Township sixteen South, Range twenty five East, Section thirty five, which includes the south half of the south half of that section. Map means a drawing that shows both pieces of land that will be traded, as agreed to by the Centers and Caza Ranches LLC. Non federal land means about one hundred sixty acres of land owned by Caza Ranches LLC. This land is shown on the Map but does not include any underground rights owned by other parties. This land is identified as parcel number four dash one four seven dash zero nine seven dash one three two dash one three two. The legal description is Township seventeen South, Range twenty five East, Section four, which includes the northwest quarter of that section. Secretary means the Secretary of Homeland Security.

The actual land exchange works like this. When Caza Ranches LLC offers to give the Secretary all of their ownership rights to the non federal land, the Secretary is allowed to accept this offer. The Secretary can then give Caza Ranches LLC all of the United States ownership rights to the federal land. The title to the land that Caza Ranches LLC gives to the Secretary must meet the same standards that the Attorney General of the United States requires for all government land purchases.

The land exchange has several rules and requirements. The exchange must respect any valid existing rights that were in place when this law was passed. The Secretary can add any extra terms and conditions that protect the interests of the United States, including protecting those existing rights. The costs of completing this land exchange will be split equally between the Secretary and Caza Ranches LLC. The value of the lands being traded is considered to be equal. All terms and conditions for the land exchange must be written down in an exchange agreement that both Caza Ranches LLC and the Secretary sign.

Once the Secretary accepts ownership of the non federal land, several things happen. The non federal land becomes part of the Centers and will be managed as part of the Centers. The Centers will use this land to build structures that support the Federal Law Enforcement Training Centers training mission. The boundaries of the Centers will be changed to remove the federal land that was traded away.

Both the Secretary and Caza Ranches LLC can make small changes by mutual agreement. They can make minor boundary adjustments to the federal and non federal lands involved in the exchange. They can also fix any small errors in the Map or any other map, size estimate, or legal description of the lands. If there is a disagreement between a map, a size estimate, or a legal description of the lands involved in the exchange, the Map will be the final authority unless the Secretary and Caza Ranches LLC both agree to something different. The Secretary must file and make available for public viewing at the New Mexico headquarters of the Department of Homeland Security a copy of the Map and any other maps mentioned in this section.
